Which part of a plant helps the plant make food from sunlight?
A tree uses gas A to produce gas B. An animal uses gas B to produce gas A. What is gas B in this cycle?

A plant scientist is developing a new fertilizer called Hypergrow. She designs an experiment to test whether Hypergrow helps plants grow faster than Supergrow, a fertilizer already on the market. Which of the following should the scientist do with light during the experiment?
facts.truth: Apply the same amount of light to all the plants.
distractors: Apply only green light to all the plants.
Flash the lights to simulate a partly cloudy day.

facts.context: Radioactive waste from nuclear power plants is sealed in containers and deeply buried in the ground. Sealing and burying waste reduces the chances that water will become contaminated. At which point in the water cycle would radioactive contamination most likely be found?
Research has shown that specialization of stem cells into specific cell types results, in part, from cell shape. What provides a specialized cell with its shape?
Which statement best describes a bacterial cell?
distractors: It contains more than one cell.
It is part of a larger organism.
It contains different kinds of cells.

facts.context: How do sprouting plants exhibit positive gravitropism?
distractors: The leaves face toward the Sun.

distractors: a bone on top of a sand pile

Why is the particular sequence of bases in a segment of DNA important to cells?
facts.truth: Some base sequences code for protein production.
distractors: Some base sequences cause the release of lipids from the nucleus.
Some base sequences contain the order of sugars in polysaccharides.
Some base sequences produce electrical signals sent to the cytoplasm.
